mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and liquidity support for risk assets, so declines in the policy rate predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating at the same time.
failure_condition: If 20d forward SPX returns are not higher after monthly FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising rates, or if the sign relationship flips for two consecutive 3-year rolling windows, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE marks a worsening labor-market shock that reduces income expectations and broadens recession risk, implying negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should appear when unemployment is rising faster than the recent trend.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after UNRATE rises is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A recovery in RSAFS indicates improving household spending and revenue momentum, which should lift earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when sales accelerate after a prior weakness.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive on a 3-month change basis
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS changes are not followed by above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the rank correlation stays below 0.03 in any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: An improving INDPRO trend implies stronger real activity and better cyclical profit expectations, so positive monthly industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect should be strongest when output is recovering from contraction.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO monthly changes fail to improve 5d forward returns relative to negative changes, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises while PAYEMS also rises, the combination signals simultaneous improvement in housing demand and labor income, which should support equities over the next quarter. The mechanism is a joint real-activity confirmation, not either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform each single-series condition in predicting 60d SPX returns, or if joint-trigger returns are not significantly positive versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising CPIAUCSL and a still-low FEDFUNDS rate implies inflation is accelerating faster than policy normalization, increasing real-rate pressure and discount-rate uncertainty. Under that combination, S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days should be negative.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rises while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-plus-low-rate interaction does not yield lower 20d SPX returns than either proxy alone, or if the interaction coefficient is indistinguishable from zero in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series indicates labor-market deterioration and weaker future consumption, so negative payroll momentum should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect should be clearest when payrolls contract after a prior expansion.
failure_condition: Reject if negative PAYEMS monthly changes do not associate with below-baseline 5d SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate of negative forward returns is not above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a recession-recovery regime, stronger GDPC1 growth alongside stable or improving RSAFS confirms a real-demand rebound that should push equities higher over the next quarter. The signal fails when GDP growth is revised away or retail sales weaken concurrently.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth turns positive after a contraction
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDP-turnaround episodes do not produce above-median 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction loses significance after controlling for GDP revision windows.
